title: thebasics-handbook-title-notes
text: Notes provide server-side writing tools for roleplay, memory, and staff coordination. Notes open a GUI with an entry list, a selected entry editor, a freeform notes area, and Reload, Save, Close, and Guide buttons. Closing or reloading with unsaved changes asks for confirmation. Private notes Private notes are visible only to you. Use them for personal reminders, character details, or private observations about another player. Commands: - /notes: open your private notes about yourself. - /notes about <player>: open your private notes about another known player. - /notes help: show syntax. - /notes list, /notes add <text>, /notes view <id>, /notes edit <id> <text>, /notes delete <id> confirm. Aliases include /personalnotes and /pnotes. Staff notes Staff notes are shared staff-only notes about a player account. They require the configured admin notes privilege. Staff notes can include structured timestamped entries and, when enabled, a freeform staff ledger. Commands: - /adminnotes <player>: open staff notes for a player. - /adminnotes help: show syntax. - /adminnotes <player> list, add <text>, view <id>, edit <id> <text>, delete <id> confirm. - /adminnotes <player> ledger view and /adminnotes <player> ledger set <text>. Aliases include /anotes and /staffnotes. Caveats Player lookup can find offline players known to the server. Note IDs can be abbreviated only when the prefix uniquely matches. Delete commands require confirm. Staff note changes are audit-logged.
